title: Asymptotic-Möbius maps
abstract: We introduce asymptotic-Möbius (AM) maps, a large-scale analogue of quasi-Möbius maps tailored to geometric group theory. AM-maps capture coarse cross-ratio behavior for configurations of points that lie far apart, providing a notion of "conformality at infinity" that is stable under quasi-isometries, compatible with scaling limits, and rigid enough to yield structural consequences absent from Pansu's notion of large-scale conformality. We establish basic properties of AM-maps, give several sources of examples, including quasi-isometries, sublinear bi-Lipschitz equivalences, snowflaking, and Assouad embeddings, and apply the theory to large-scale dimension and metric cotype. As applications we obtain dimension-monotonicity results for nilpotent groups and CAT(0) spaces, and new obstructions to the existence of AM-maps arising from metric cotype.
